explanation:
Let's break down the requirement:

1. Modes: Applies in PCV and PSV modes ⇒ (PCVMode | PSVMode)
2. Expiratory Pause can be initiated if set by GUI ⇒ (expiratoryPauseButton)
3. Behavior: If the pause is requested at some point, then at the end of the next expiration, if it is still requested, both valves close until the pause is stopped by GUI.

LTL structure:
- Whenever (in PCV or PSV mode) and GUI requests Expiratory Pause, then after the end of the next expiration phase (expiratoryPhaseEnd), if Expiratory Pause is still requested, the ventilator closes both valves (inValveClose & outValveClose) until GUI releases the pause (¬expiratoryPauseButton).

We express 'both valves close until pause is stopped' as G((PCVMode|PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton -> X(F(expiratoryPhaseEnd & (expiratoryPauseButton U (inValveClose & outValveClose U ¬expiratoryPauseButton))))).

Step-by-step explanation:
1. PCVMode | PSVMode: we consider only these modes.
2. expiratoryPauseButton: pause requested by GUI.
3. After the next occurrence of expiratoryPhaseEnd, and if pause is still requested (expiratoryPauseButton), then both valves close (inValveClose & outValveClose), until GUI releases pause (¬expiratoryPauseButton).
4. The requirement must hold globally.

Thus, the complete LTL is:
G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton) -> X(F(expiratoryPhaseEnd & (expiratoryPauseButton & X((inValveClose & outValveClose) U ¬expiratoryPauseButton)))))

explanation_dict.natural_language: Modes: Applies in PCV and PSV modes
Expiratory Pause is set by GUI
End of next expiration phase
If ExpiratoryPause is still required, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves will close until pause is stopped by the GUI
Tie all together globally: Whenever in appropriate mode and pause requested, after next expiration end, if pause is still requested, both valves close until released
explanation_dict.LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton) -> X(F(expiratoryPhaseEnd & (expiratoryPauseButton & X((inValveClose & outValveClose) U ¬expiratoryPauseButton)))))

output_LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton) -> X(F(expiratoryPhaseEnd & (expiratoryPauseButton & X((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !expiratoryPauseButton)))))

explanation: We focus on these requirements: (1) In PCV or PSV mode, if the GUI sets Expiratory Pause, the ventilator must (a) wait for the end of the next expiration, (b) check if Expiratory Pause is still required, and (c) if so, close both in and out valves (inValveClose and outValveClose) until the GUI stops the Expiratory Pause. We use operators as follows: 'if' maps to ->, 'wait for the end of next expiration' maps to finding the next expiratoryPhaseEnd, 'still required' means expiratoryPauseButton is still true, 'close both valves' maps to inValveClose & outValveClose, and 'until pause is stopped' means this holds until not expiraotryPauseButton. The whole behavior must only apply in PCVMode or PSVMode, so we use the globally (G) operator with a leading PCVMode or PSVMode condition. The core sequence is captured by temporal operators: when Expiratory Pause is requested (expiratoryPauseButton) in PCVMode or PSVMode, then after the next expiratoryPhaseEnd, if still required, both valves are closed until the GUI clears Expiratory Pause (¬expiratoryPauseButton).

Expiratory Pause requested by GUI
Wait for end of next expiration
Both inspiratory and expiratory valves close
Valves remain closed until Expiratory Pause cleared

output_LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton) -> (F(expiratoryPhaseEnd & X(expiratoryPauseButton -> ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!expiratoryPauseButton))))))

explanation:
First, the mode must be PCVMode or PSVMode, i.e., (PCVMode | PSVMode). The key event that triggers the behavior is when the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI (expiratoryPauseButton). According to the requirement, *after* the button is set, the ventilator should wait for the end of the next expiration phase (i.e., expiratoryPhaseEnd), then, if the Expiratory Pause is still required (expiratoryPause), then inspiratory and expiratory valves must close (inValveClose & outValveClose) until the Expiratory Pause is stopped (i.e., not expiratoryPause anymore). This suggests a chain: Whenever we are in the right mode and expiratoryPauseButton becomes true, eventually, after the next expiratoryPhaseEnd, if expiratoryPause still holds, inValveClose & outValveClose is set and stays so until not expiratoryPause. The main logic is:

Globally, whenever (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton, it is required that after the next expiratoryPhaseEnd, if expiratoryPause, both inValveClose and outValveClose hold until !expiratoryPause.

The translation is:
G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton) -> F(expiratoryPhaseEnd & F(expiratoryPause -> ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !expiratoryPause))))

This means: always, if Expiratory Pause is requested by the GUI in PCV or PSV mode, then eventually (i.e., after at least one expiratory phase end), if Expiratory Pause is still active, then from that moment, both valves close until Expiratory Pause ends. The "wait for the end of the next expiration" is reflected by the first F(expiratoryPhaseEnd ...), and the closing of valves is bounded by the duration of Expiratory Pause.

Note: For a more precise "at the first expiratoryPhaseEnd after Button was set", a more complex pattern like introducing a trigger variable might be needed, but the formula above captures the described behavior for LTL.

explanation_dict.natural_language: PCV or PSV mode is active
Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI
Global requirement (always)
After Expiratory Pause is set, wait for the next expiration end
If Expiratory Pause is still active after the next expiration, set both valves closed until pause is stopped

output_LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton) -> F(expiratoryPhaseEnd & F(expiratoryPause -> ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !expiratoryPause))))

explanation:
We are capturing the requirements for Expiratory Pause in PCV and PSV modes. The essential behavior is: (1) If in PCV or PSV mode AND Expiratory Pause is required by the GUI (expiratoryPauseButton), then, after the current expiratory phase ends (expiratoryPhaseEnd), if the pause is still required, both valves are closed (inValveClose & outValveClose) until the pause is stopped (¬expiratoryPauseButton).

To formalize this in LTL:
1. '(PCVMode or PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton' means Expiratory Pause is required and system is in PCV or PSV mode. 
2. 'wait for the end of the next expiration phase' means after the next expiratoryPhaseEnd after the button is pressed.
3. After that, if the pause is still set, both valves close. The closure persists until the pause is stopped by the GUI (i.e., until ¬expiratoryPauseButton).

We express this with:
Globally, if we enter a situation where (PCVMode or PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton & expiratoryPhase, then, at the next expiratoryPhaseEnd (the end of current expiratory phase),
  if expiratoryPauseButton still holds, then from that moment, both valves shall be closed until the pause is stopped.

This is modeled in LTL as:
G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton & expiratoryPhase & X(expiratoryPhaseEnd)) -> X( expiratoryPauseButton -> ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!expiratoryPauseButton) ) ))
That is: if we're in Expiratory Pause state at the start of expiration, and at the end of expiration the pause is still required, then both valves close until the button is released.

This abstracts away details like overlapping pause requests and multiple subsequent expirations, but covers the described logic per requirement.

During Expiratory Phase

Both inspiratory and expiratory valves close

Globally, whenever those conditions hold

output_LTL: G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton & expiratoryPhase & X(expiratoryPhaseEnd)) -> X( expiratoryPauseButton -> ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!expiratoryPauseButton) ) ))

explanation:
The requirement can be broken down into several pieces. First, 'in PCV and PSV modes' means the requirement only applies globally when (PCVMode or PSVMode) is true. Then, 'there shall be the possibility to initiate an Expiratory Pause if it is set by the GUI' is a possibility property; but the core behavior is described afterwards. Specifically:

- If the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI (expiratoryPauseButton is set),
- The ventilator shall wait for the end of the next expiration phase (expiratoryPhaseEnd occurs),
- If the Expiratory Pause is still required (expiratoryPauseButton is still set),
- Then both the inspiratory and expiratory valves are closed (inValveClose and outValveClose) until Expiratory Pause is stopped by the GUI (¬expiratoryPauseButton).

Thus, whenever (PCVMode or PSVMode) and at the moment expiratoryPauseButton becomes true, in all future points, after the next expiratoryPhaseEnd, if expiratoryPauseButton is still true, then (inValveClose & outValveClose) holds until ¬expiratoryPauseButton.

The LTL encoding uses triggers for the expiration phase end and initiation by GUI. We express that whenever in PCV/PSV and Expiratory Pause was set, after the next expiratoryPhaseEnd, as long as expiratoryPauseButton is true, the valves remain closed until it is unset. 

We encode this as:
G((PCVMode | PSVMode) -> G((expiratoryPauseButton & X(F expiratoryPhaseEnd)) -> X((expiratoryPhaseEnd -> (expiratoryPauseButton -> X((expiratoryPauseButton) U (¬expiratoryPauseButton & inValveClose & outValveClose)))))))

However, to simplify: any time, if in PCVMode or PSVMode and expiratoryPauseButton becomes true, at every expiratoryPhaseEnd, if still in Expiratory Pause, inValveClose & outValveClose should hold until Expiratory Pause is stopped:
G((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton & expiratoryPhaseEnd -> ((expiratoryPauseButton) U (¬expiratoryPauseButton & inValveClose & outValveClose)))

But, because the requirement is: after Expiratory Pause requested, at the next expiratoryPhaseEnd, then the Pause state (valves close) holds as long as button is held, so the main requirement can be approximated as:
G((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton & X expiratoryPhaseEnd -> X((expiratoryPauseButton) U (¬expiratoryPauseButton & inValveClose & outValveClose)))

The 'pause' (valves closed) should be initiated after the next expiratoryPhaseEnd, so the effect starts in the position after the end of expiration, so we use 'next (X)' operator after expiratoryPhaseEnd.

Final translation:
G((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton & X expiratoryPhaseEnd -> X((expiratoryPauseButton) U (¬expiratoryPauseButton & inValveClose & outValveClose)))

This ensures that whenever Expiratory Pause is requested in PCV or PSV, after the end of the next expiration phase, if the pause is still requested, both valves are closed, and they stay closed until the pause is released.

explanation:
First, we identify the relevant atomic propositions: PCVMode and PSVMode (indicate the ventilator is in pressure controlled ventilation or pressure support ventilation modes), expiratoryPauseButton (the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI), expiratoryPhaseEnd (the end of expiration), expiratoryPause (the ventilator is in Expiratory Pause), inValveClose & outValveClose (inspiratory and expiratory valves closed), and a formula indicating 'Expiratory Pause is still required' (expiratoryPauseButton is still true). The requirement translates as: (1) If the ventilator is in PCV or PSV mode, and the user sets expiratory pause (expiratoryPauseButton), then after the end of the next expiration phase (expiratoryPhaseEnd), if the pause is still requested, both valves close (inValveClose & outValveClose), and this stays true until the pause is stopped (¬expiratoryPauseButton).

Formal steps:
1. 'In PCV or PSV mode' → PCVMode | PSVMode
2. 'expiratoryPauseButton' is pressed (Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI).
3. 'Wait for the end of the next expiration phase': 'after' means 'next time expiratoryPhaseEnd' after the setting.
4. 'If still required' → still expiratoryPauseButton at that point.
5. 'Both the inspiratory and expiratory valves will close until the expiratoryPause is stopped': (inValveClose & outValveClose) holds while expiratoryPauseButton holds, starting at that time, until ¬expiratoryPauseButton.

This is most precisely captured as:
Globally, if (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton, then after the next expiratoryPhaseEnd, if expiratoryPauseButton still holds, then (inValveClose & outValveClose) holds until ¬expiratoryPauseButton.

Translated to LTL:
G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton) -> X((!(expiratoryPhaseEnd) U (expiratoryPhaseEnd & expiratoryPauseButton & ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !expiratoryPauseButton)))))
This means: whenever the system is in PCV or PSV mode and expiratoryPauseButton is pressed, from the NEXT moment, wait until expiratoryPhaseEnd happens, and if pause is still requested, close both valves until the pause is stopped.

Wait for end of the next expiration phase
If the Expiratory Pause is still required (button still set)
Both inValve and outValve are closed until the pause is stopped
Globally applies to the requirement

output_LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton) -> X((!(expiratoryPhaseEnd) U (expiratoryPhaseEnd & expiratoryPauseButton & ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !expiratoryPauseButton)))))

explanation:
We want to capture that: In PCV or PSV mode, if the user sets Expiratory Pause (via the GUI), then at the end of the next expiration phase, if Expiratory Pause is still required, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves should be closed until the pause is stopped by the GUI.

Let us choose atomic propositions:
- PCVMode, PSVMode: the two modes.
- expiratoryPauseButton: the pause is required/set by GUI.
- expiratoryPhaseEnd: end of expiration phase (marks when to latch the pause logic).
- inValveClose, outValveClose: inspiratory/expiratory valves are closed.
- expiratoryPause: the pause is ongoing.

High-level logic:
1. If in PCV/PSV mode, when the user sets expiratory pause (expiratoryPauseButton), after the *end* of the next expiration (expiratoryPhaseEnd), if the user *still* requires this (expiratoryPauseButton is still true), then both inValveClose and outValveClose are true (valves close) and stay so until the user unsets the pause (¬expiratoryPauseButton).

We can model this as:
Globally, if (in PCV or PSV mode) and expiratoryPauseButton becomes true, then after the end of the next expiration, if expiratoryPauseButton is still true, then (inValveClose ∧ outValveClose) holds until expiratoryPauseButton is unset.

The phrase 'after *the end* of the next expiration' requires us to wait for the *following* expiratoryPhaseEnd after the pause is requested.

So we define:
G ( ( (PCVMode ∨ PSVMode) ∧ expiratoryPauseButton ) →
    (X (¬expiratoryPhaseEnd U (expiratoryPhaseEnd ∧ X ( expiratoryPauseButton → ( (inValveClose ∧ outValveClose) U ¬expiratoryPauseButton ) ))))
)

- The X (¬expiratoryPhaseEnd U expiratoryPhaseEnd ...) part says: After the condition becomes true, wait until you see the next expiratoryPhaseEnd.
- At the expiratoryPhaseEnd, on the next step, check if the button is still pressed (expiratoryPauseButton). If so, enter the pause logic.
- While the button is still pressed, both valves are closed (hold as invariant until the pause is released).

Note: For compactness, some environmental constraints (e.g., safety/failsafe overrides) are not encoded as they are not mentioned in the requirement.

explanation_dict.natural_language: In PCV or PSV mode
Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI
wait for the end of the next expiration phase
if the Pause is still required, both valves close until the pause is stopped
Globally

output_LTL: G( ( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton ) -> X( (!expiratoryPhaseEnd) U (expiratoryPhaseEnd & X( expiratoryPauseButton -> ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !expiratoryPauseButton) ) ) ) )

explanation:
We identify the key requirements: 1) In PCV or PSV mode and if an expiratory pause is enabled by the user (via the GUI, i.e., expiratoryPauseButton), when an expiration phase starts, after reaching the end of this expiration phase if the pause is still required (expiratoryPause holds), both inspiratory and expiratory valves shall close (inValveClose & outValveClose) and this state is maintained until the pause is cancelled by the GUI (¬expiratoryPauseButton or ¬expiratoryPause). 
1. PCV or PSV mode can be formalized as (PCVMode | PSVMode).
2. Expiratory pause can be requested by the GUI (expiratoryPauseButton).
3. When an expiration phase starts (expiratoryPhaseStart), from the next expiration phase end (expiratoryPhaseEnd), we check if the pause is still required (expiratoryPause holds).
4. If so, then both inValveClose and outValveClose must continuously hold until the pause is cancelled.
5. The overall property is globally (G): If (in PCV or PSV mode) and GUI requests a pause at some moment, then after the following expiratory phase end, if the pause is still required, both valves must remain closed until the GUI cancels the pause (¬expiratoryPause), i.e., as long as the pause is required.

This can be formalized as:
G ( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton & X F expiratoryPhaseEnd & X (F (expiratoryPhaseEnd & expiratoryPause)) ) -> F ( (expiratoryPause & expiratoryPhaseEnd) -> ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U (¬expiratoryPause) ) ) )

Alternatively, we can simplify by focusing on the main causal chain:
Globally, if in PCV/PSV mode and the pause is required (expiratoryPause), and at the end of an expiratory phase (expiratoryPhaseEnd), then both valves close until the pause is cancelled.
So,
G ( ( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPause & expiratoryPhaseEnd ) -> ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U (¬expiratoryPause) ) )

This formula says: at any time, when we are in PCV or PSV, an expiratory pause is required, and the expiration phase ends, both valves close and remain so until the pause is no longer required.

Expiratory pause is still required
At the end of expiratory phase
Both valves close until pause is stopped
Globally, in PCV/PSV, if pause is required after expiratory phase end, both valves close until pause ends
explanation_dict.LTL: G( ( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPause & expiratoryPhaseEnd ) -> ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U (¬expiratoryPause) ) )

output_LTL: G( ( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPause & expiratoryPhaseEnd ) -> ( (inValveClose & outValveClose) U (¬expiratoryPause) ) )

explanation:
To translate the sentence: 'In PCV and PSV modes there shall be the possibility to initiate an Expiratory Pause if it is set by the GUI: When the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI, the ventilator shall wait for the end of the next expiration phase, and if the Expiratory Pause is still required, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves will close until the expiratory pause is stopped by the GUI.'

Step 1: Consider only PCV and PSV modes. These are indicated by atomic propositions 'PCVMode' and 'PSVMode'.

Step 2: 'ExpiratoryPause is set by the GUI' is indicated by 'expiratoryPauseButton'.

Step 3: After the 'expiratoryPauseButton', the ventilator waits for the next 'expiratoryPhaseEnd'.

Step 4: If Expiratory Pause is still required after the expiration ends, that is, 'expiratoryPauseButton' (was not cleared), we initiate both valves closing: 'inValveClose & outValveClose'.

Step 5: The valves remain closed until 'expiratoryPauseButton' is deactivated (we assume this means it's no longer asserted).

Temporal pattern:
- Globally, in PCV or PSV mode, whenever expiratoryPauseButton is pressed, after the next expiratoryPhaseEnd, if the button is still pressed, both valves are closed until the button is released.

We can encode a "wait for next expiratoryPhaseEnd" using the 'U' (until) operator. The condition 'inValveClose & outValveClose' holds starting right after the first expiratoryPhaseEnd after expiratoryPauseButton is set, and for as long as expiratoryPauseButton remains true. When it becomes false, the requirement ends.

To express this in LTL:
1) We watch for: (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton
2) From that point, after the next expiratoryPhaseEnd, if the button is still pressed, then both valves remain closed until !expiratoryPauseButton.

In LTL, this is written as:
G( ( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton ) -> X ( (true U expiratoryPhaseEnd) -> (expiratoryPauseButton & G( (expiratoryPauseButton) -> (inValveClose & outValveClose) ) U ( !expiratoryPauseButton ) ) ) )

Simplify: Once the button is pressed in correct mode, after the next expiratoryPhaseEnd, if still pressed, then both valves are closed until the button is unpressed. Since we're just required to keep 'inValveClose & outValveClose' as long as the button remains pressed after the next expiration end, we can simplify:

G( ( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton ) -> X( (true U expiratoryPhaseEnd) -> (expiratoryPauseButton & ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !expiratoryPauseButton) ) ) )

If you want to make it so that 'inValveClose & outValveClose' start immediately after 'expiratoryPhaseEnd' if the button is still pressed, and continue until it is released, that's what the 'U' is doing here.

Note: If you must be more precise about the boundary between detection and action (e.g., at the next state after expiratoryPhaseEnd), consider adding additional 'X' operators, but for most LTL semantics this construction suffices.

Expiratory Pause is set by GUI
wait for next expiratoryPhaseEnd
if still Expiratory Pause, both valves close until Expiratory Pause stops
Globally, the above holds when Expiratory Pause is set in PCV/PSV mode

output_LTL: G( ( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton ) -> X( (true U expiratoryPhaseEnd) -> (expiratoryPauseButton & ((inValveClose & outValveClose) U !expiratoryPauseButton) ) ) )

explanation:
The sentence specifies that: (a) In PCV or PSV modes (PCVMode or PSVMode), when an Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI (expiratoryPauseButton), the ventilator waits for the end of the next expiration phase (expiratoryPhaseEnd), and, if the Expiratory Pause is still required (expiratoryPauseButton), both the inspiratory and expiratory valves close (inValveClose & outValveClose), until the Expiratory Pause is stopped by the GUI (!expiratoryPauseButton). 

To express this in LTL:
1. The situation is only relevant in PCV or PSV mode: (PCVMode | PSVMode).
2. The trigger is the setting of Expiratory Pause by the GUI: expiratoryPauseButton.
3. After expiratoryPauseButton becomes true, the next expiratoryPhaseEnd marks when to check for holding the pause.
4. If at that time expiratoryPauseButton is still true, both valves should close until the pause is released (expiratoryPauseButton becomes false).

We encode this by: 
Globally, whenever Expiratory Pause is set in PCV or PSV mode and expiration ends, if the pause is still on, both valves close until the pause is turned off.

Let E = (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton & X(F expiratoryPhaseEnd), which captures that the system is in one of those modes, the button is set, and we await the end of the next expiration.

But to connect all, we need to enforce: Whenever (PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton becomes true, after the next expiratoryPhaseEnd, if expiratoryPauseButton is true, then from then on (as long as expiratoryPauseButton) both valves are closed, until !expiratoryPauseButton.

Thus, a concise LTL expression for the core requirement is:
G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton & X(F(expiratoryPhaseEnd))) -> 
  X(F( expiratoryPhaseEnd & executepause )) )
Where executepause stands for: (expiratoryPauseButton -> (inValveClose & outValveClose)) U !expiratoryPauseButton
But to capture that, we split sub-formula as below.

explanation_dict.natural_language: System is in PCV or PSV mode
Expiratory Pause is set by GUI
Wait for the end of the next expiration phase
At expiration end, if pause is still set, close both valves until pause is released
Globally, whenever mode & pause button set, upon next expiration end (and pause still set), close both valves until pause released
explanation_dict.LTL: G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton & X(F expiratoryPhaseEnd)) -> X(F( expiratoryPhaseEnd & (expiratoryPauseButton -> (inValveClose & outValveClose)) U !expiratoryPauseButton )))

output_LTL: G( ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & expiratoryPauseButton & X(F expiratoryPhaseEnd)) -> X(F( expiratoryPhaseEnd & ((expiratoryPauseButton -> (inValveClose & outValveClose)) U !expiratoryPauseButton) )))
